text:
CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old female with newly identified bilateral ovarian masses with peritoneal carcinomatosis and omental caking on concurrent abdominal/pelvic CT. Staging evaluation to assess for intrathoracic metastatic disease. Elevated CA-125 at 1,847 U/mL.

TECHNIQUE: Axial CT of the chest performed with intravenous iodinated contrast following standard power injection protocol. Reformatted images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Airways:
The central airways are patent. The trachea is midline without deviation or narrowing. The main, lobar, and segmental bronchi are widely patent bilaterally. No endobronchial lesions identified. The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses are identified. No ground-glass opacity, consolidation, or interstitial abnormality. No centrilobular nodularity. No tree-in-bud pattern. Lung parenchyma is otherwise unremarkable without evidence of emphysema, fibrosis, or air trapping.

Pleura:
No pleural effusion identified bilaterally. Trace bilateral pleural thickening is noted, most prominent along the posterior-lateral mid thorax bilaterally, measuring up to 2-3 mm in thickness. No discrete pleural nodularity or nodular pleural implants. The appearance is nonspecific and likely reactive, given the known large volume of ascites and inflammatory/neoplastic peritoneal disease. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum:
The mediastinum is unremarkable in configuration and density. No mediastinal widening. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal mass. The thymus is not enlarged. No pericardial effusion. No pericardial thickening.

Cardiac:
The heart is normal in size. Cardiac contours are smooth. No intracardiac or pericardial masses. Coronary artery calcification is absent. The great vessels are normal in caliber. The aorta and pulmonary arteries are of normal size and enhancement pattern. No filling defects within the visualized pulmonary arteries to suggest acute pulmonary embolism, though this examination was not specifically optimized for PE protocol.

Lymphadenopathy:
No mediastinal lymph node enlargement. No hilar lymphadenopathy. Subcentimeter prevascular, precarinal, and bilateral paratracheal lymph nodes are identified, all within normal size limits and without morphologically suspicious features (no rounded or necrotic nodes). No supraclavicular adenopathy on the included images.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues:
Chest wall musculature is symmetric and unremarkable. No subcutaneous nodules. No axillary lymphadenopathy on the visualized portions bilaterally.

Osseous Structures:
The visualized thoracic spine, ribs, sternum, clavicles, and shoulder girdles are intact. No focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified. No pathologic fractures. Bone density appears preserved for stated age.

Upper Abdomen (included on scout and inferior slices):
Very limited evaluation of the upper abdomen is available at the inferior extent of the scan. No discrete hepatic dome masses are identified on this limited view, though complete evaluation of hepatic surface implants is deferred to the concurrent dedicated abdominopelvic CT.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or parenchymal lesions to suggest intrathoracic metastatic disease.

2. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

3. No pleural effusion. Trace bilateral pleural thickening, most likely reactive in the setting of known large-volume ascites and peritoneal disease; no discrete pleural nodularity to suggest pleural implants.

4. No pericardial effusion.

5. No osseous lesions suspicious for metastatic involvement.

6. Taken together with the concurrent abdominal and pelvic CT demonstrating bilateral ovarian masses with extensive peritoneal carcinomatosis, these findings are most consistent with disease confined to the abdomen and pelvis without intrathoracic involvement. This supports FIGO stage IIIC classification rather than stage IV disease. Clinical and pathologic correlation recommended.

Signed electronically.
Attending Radiologist
